triggers: I need to see the doctor
I want to make an appointment
script: Of course — I can help schedule that. Is this for a new concern or a follow-up? What is the main reason for the visit? I have availability on Tuesday at 10:30 AM or Thursday at 2:00 PM. Which works better for you? Perfect — I've scheduled you for Tuesday at 10:30 AM. You'll receive a confirmation message shortly. If anything changes, you can reply 'RESCHEDULE' or call us.
hard_stop: If patient mentions chest pain, shortness of breath, stroke symptoms: I'm concerned this may be urgent. Please call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

triggers: I need to change my appointment
script: No problem — would you like to reschedule or cancel? If reschedule, same live scheduling logic. If cancel, remove appointment and confirm.

triggers: I'd like to become a new patient
I want to schedule my first appointment
script: Welcome. I'd be happy to help schedule your first visit with Dr. Soliman. What is the main reason for your appointment so we can schedule the appropriate visit type?
hard_stop: IF urgent symptoms → route to urgent triage.

triggers: The doctor asked me to return
script: I'd be happy to schedule your follow-up appointment. Would you prefer in-person or telemedicine?

triggers: Patient reports symptoms requiring same-day evaluation
script: I understand this may require prompt attention. Let me check for a same-day appointment. IF same-day slot available → schedule urgent visit. IF no slot → escalate to physician or recommend urgent care.
hard_stop: If emergency symptoms: Please seek immediate care at the emergency room.

script: We can schedule a telemedicine visit so you can speak with the doctor from home. You will receive a secure video link before the appointment.
hard_stop: Not allowed for: physical procedures, physical exams requiring examination.

script: I'll schedule your procedure appointment and provide preparation instructions.

triggers: I need to cancel my appointment
script: I can cancel that appointment for you. Would you like to reschedule at a different time?

triggers: I need to move my appointment
script: No problem. Let me check the next available times.

triggers: Patient did not arrive for scheduled appointment
script: We noticed you missed your appointment today. If you would like to reschedule, we'd be happy to assist.
hard_stop: If repeated no-shows: flag patient account, apply clinic policy if required.

triggers: Patient wants earlier appointment but schedule is full
script: I can place you on the waitlist. If an earlier appointment becomes available, we will notify you.

triggers: Patient says I'm not feeling well
script: I'm sorry you're not feeling well. Can you describe what's happening in one sentence? When did it start? Are you having chest pain, shortness of breath, severe pain, weakness, or fainting? If no red flags: I can schedule you for evaluation. If red flag: emergency script.
actions: Route to triage queue OR urgent scheduling slot
hard_stop: If red flag symptoms: route to emergency script.

script: Thank you — I'll process this request. You'll receive confirmation once reviewed.
For controlled medications, the physician must review early refill requests. I'll route this for review.

triggers: Patient requests medication refill via portal or call
script: Which medication are you requesting? Which pharmacy should we send it to? If eligible (per refill logic) → auto-approve. If not → route to physician.
May I ask which specialist you'd like to see and the reason for referral? If Medicare/PPO: I'll generate your referral and send it to you securely.
HMO referrals require insurance authorization. I'll submit this for processing and notify you once approved.
actions: Create referral task for Office Manager

script: Are you calling about an invoice, copay/deductible, or a refund? What was the date of service?
I can help with that. Would you like this sent through the secure portal? If school/work form → verify identity.

triggers: Lab marked abnormal but not critical
script: Hi, this is calling from Soliman Care. Your recent lab results showed some values slightly outside the normal range. The doctor recommends a follow-up visit to review and discuss next steps. If patient asks details: The doctor will review everything with you during the appointment.
hard_stop: If patient reports new severe symptoms → escalate.

script: This is urgent. The doctor needs to speak with you immediately. If unreachable: Call x3, Send urgent SMS: Please contact the office immediately. If symptoms present: Please go to the nearest emergency room.
hard_stop: If symptoms present: Please go to the nearest emergency room.

script: Please describe what's happening. If chest pain, SOB, stroke symptoms: This may be an emergency. Please call 911 immediately. If non-emergent: Offer urgent same-day visit.
hard_stop: If chest pain, shortness of breath, stroke symptoms: This may be an emergency. Please call 911 immediately.

script: Your refill has been sent to your pharmacy. Please let us know if you notice any side effects.
I've sent a short refill to keep you covered. Please complete your lab work as soon as possible to continue safely.
actions: Log as short refill due to labs

script: I've sent a short refill and scheduled a reminder for your follow-up visit to keep everything on track.
Controlled medications require direct physician review. I've routed your request for prompt attention. No timeline guarantees.

triggers: Can you refill my pain medication
script: For controlled medications, the doctor must review each request individually. I'll route this for review right away. No promise. No timing guarantees.
I've sent a short refill to prevent interruption. Let's schedule your follow-up to review your care plan.
This refill requires physician review to ensure safety. We'll follow up with next steps. If inappropriate: This medication cannot be refilled without an updated evaluation.
Thank you for the update. I'll send your prescription to the new pharmacy.

I'm sorry you're experiencing that. When did this start? Are you having trouble breathing, severe swelling, chest pain, or fainting? If severe → emergency escalation. If mild: I'll notify the doctor so we can advise you safely.
hard_stop: If trouble breathing, severe swelling, chest pain, fainting: Please call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

triggers: I have a question about my condition
Can I ask the doctor something
script: I'm happy to help route your question. May I confirm your full name and date of birth? Can you briefly describe your question? If purely administrative → route to correct department. If clinical: I'll send this to the clinical team for review.
I'll check whether your results have been reviewed. If reviewed & non-critical: The doctor has reviewed them. I can schedule a visit to discuss. If pending: Your results are still pending review.
Our office is currently closed. If this is a medical emergency, call 911. If non-emergent: I'll forward this to the on-call provider.
hard_stop: If medical emergency: call 911.

script: Which medication and which pharmacy? Run refill safety logic.
Your medication requires insurance authorization. We are working on this and will keep you updated.
actions: Pull chart data (ICD-10, prior meds, labs)

script: To verify your benefits, I'll need the insurance member ID and group number. May I send a secure link for you to upload a photo of your insurance card?
“I’m sorry that your experience did not meet expectations.” “We truly value your feedback and want to address this appropriately.”

triggers: Escalate to office manager if unresolved
script: “Let’s review the situation so we can resolve it constructively.” “Our goal is to ensure the best patient care and team collaboration.”
actions: Escalate to office manager if unresolved

script: “I understand your concern, and we want to make sure your experience improves.” “Let’s see how we can help address this.”
“Our membership plans are designed to provide enhanced services and benefits.” Explain: - monthly cost - included services - appointment priority if applicable

“Our practice policies are designed to ensure fair scheduling and patient care.” Then explain policy clearly. Example: “If an appointment is missed without notice, a fee may apply because that time was reserved for your care.”

triggers: “What is the price of this service?”
script: “I can provide an estimate based on the service type.” Explain: - visit cost range - insurance variability - possible additional services Important Rule Never guarantee final cost.
“Thank you for bringing this to attention. I will escalate this to the appropriate supervisor so it can be reviewed promptly.”
“Thank you for reaching out. We will make every effort to assist you promptly.” Priority Handling - faster scheduling - direct physician communication if needed - concierge workflow
“A required document appears incomplete. Please review and complete the documentation.”
“Please contact the patient to schedule follow-up labs or appointment as instructed.”
“I’m routing this message to the nursing team for clinical review.”
“This request requires insurance authorization before proceeding.”
“This matter requires physician review. I will forward it to the doctor.”
“Please coordinate the referral and notify the patient once scheduled.”
“Please coordinate scheduling to ensure the patient receives all required services.”
“I’m assigning this task so it can be completed promptly.” Task Information Task must include: - patient name - task description - priority level - responsible staff role - due date

triggers: “Why do I have to pay a copay?”
“I thought my insurance covered this visit.”
script: (Simple Language) “A copay is the portion of the visit cost that your insurance requires you to pay at the time of service.” “The amount is determined by your insurance plan.” If Copay Type Clarification Needed Explain categories: - Primary care visit copay - Specialist copay - Telemedicine copay

triggers: “Why am I charged for this service?”
script: “CPT codes are standardized codes used to describe medical services provided during your visit.” Example: 99214 “This code represents an established patient visit that required moderate medical decision-making.” Important Rule Do not explain medical diagnosis unless already documented and visible to patient.

triggers: Route to billing specialist if appeal possible
script: “Your insurance reviewed the claim and determined it was not covered under your current plan.” Possible reasons: - Service not covered - Prior authorization required - Deductible not met - Coding issue
actions: Route to billing specialist if appeal possible

script: “We noticed you missed your appointment today. Would you like to reschedule?” If policy applies: “A no-show fee may apply per office policy.” Action - Apply fee (if policy) - Offer reschedule - Log contact

triggers: “I can’t pay the full amount right now.”
script: “We understand medical expenses can be difficult. We may be able to set up a payment plan.” Plan Options (Example) - Monthly payment arrangement - Partial payment today - Scheduled billing Compliance Rule Payment plans must follow practice policy. Doctor Twin cannot approve special financial arrangements beyond policy.
“A refund may occur if your insurance paid more than expected or if an overpayment was made.” Investigation Steps Check: - Insurance adjustments - Duplicate payment - Billing correction

triggers: “I received a bill and don’t understand it.”
script: “I’m happy to review the statement with you.” “This statement shows the services provided, what your insurance paid, and the remaining balance.”
“I’m sorry you’re experiencing a portal issue. Let’s try to resolve it.” Troubleshooting Steps 1️⃣ Confirm patient login status 2️⃣ Check portal service status 3️⃣ Offer alternate payment options
Generate patient-friendly summary: diagnosis, medications, next steps, follow-up. Attach to portal/app. Allow physician edit before sending.
Based on documentation: suggest visit level (e.g. 99214) and diagnosis codes. Doctor Twin suggests only; physician confirms final codes.
hard_stop: Doctor Twin suggests only. Physician confirms final codes.

script: Display patient-friendly explanation matching physician plan and verified medical sources. Example: High blood pressure increases the risk of heart disease and stroke. Managing it with medication, diet, and exercise can significantly reduce those risks.
hard_stop: Education must match physician plan and verified medical sources.

script: A follow-up visit in three months is typically recommended. Suggest timeline and offer scheduling.
Let's confirm your current medications. Compare chart list, patient-reported, and pharmacy fill history. Highlight discrepancies.

triggers: Diagnosis or symptoms discussed during visit
script: Based on this condition, these labs are typically monitored. Orders appear as suggestions, not auto-submitted.
hard_stop: Orders appear as suggestions, not auto-submitted.

script: Generate summary panel: patient demographics, problem list, last visit summary, current medications, recent labs/imaging, outstanding tasks. Highlight abnormal labs and flag overdue screenings.

triggers: Doctor dictates or enters encounter notes
script: Auto-populate SOAP template (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan). Insert structured vitals and labs. Await physician confirmation.
hard_stop: Doctor Twin never finalizes the note. Physician must review and approve.

script: Front desk: schedule follow-up in 3 months. Send task to staff dashboard and track completion.
“These forms typically require physician review. Please upload the paperwork through the secure portal.”
“I can provide a copy of your immunization record.” “You’ll receive a downloadable document through the patient portal.”
“I can prepare a medical note confirming your visit.”

triggers: “I need a copy of my medical records.”
“I want my lab results and notes.”
Patient portal identity if digital request
script: “I can help you obtain a copy of your medical records.” “For security purposes, we’ll need to verify your identity and confirm how you would like to receive the records.”
“Thank you for your request. We will process the records once a valid authorization form is received.”
“To release your records, we need a signed authorization form specifying the recipient.”

triggers: Notify physician if clinical relevance detected
script: “Your document has been received and added to your medical record.” “If physician review is required, we will notify you.” Processing Steps Classify uploaded document: - Lab result - Imaging report - Insurance document - Other clinical records
actions: Notify physician if clinical relevance detected

script: “Thank you for the update. We’ll schedule a follow-up within the recommended timeframe.” Create discharge follow-up task.
“I’ll confirm with the physician and return your call.” Immediate clinical task.
“Thank you. I’ll update the referral status in our system.” Update referral tracker.
“Thank you for notifying us. I’m alerting the physician immediately.” Immediate physician alert + patient contact.
Let's schedule a follow-up visit to review your imaging results.

triggers: Imaging shows finding requiring follow-up but not urgent
script: The imaging showed findings that are not urgent but require follow-up. The doctor recommends scheduling a visit to discuss next steps.
hard_stop: Avoid detailed interpretation beyond documented summary.

script: Your imaging study was reviewed and shows no concerning findings. If you have persistent symptoms, we can schedule follow-up.
The doctor recommends evaluation by a specialist. I can assist in coordinating your referral.
This imaging result requires urgent attention. If you are experiencing worsening symptoms, please seek emergency care.
hard_stop: Physician confirmation required.

script: “May I confirm which patient and date of service?” Provide minimum necessary info.
“I’ll check the status and confirm whether additional documentation is needed.” Route to PA Brain.
“Thank you for explaining the denial. We will review and determine next steps.” Log denial.
“I’ll coordinate directly with the physician to schedule the peer-to-peer discussion.” Escalate to physician immediately.

This result requires immediate medical attention. If you are experiencing symptoms such as chest pain, shortness of breath, severe weakness, or confusion, please call 911 immediately.
hard_stop: Immediate physician awareness required.

triggers: Mild elevation cholesterol A1c liver enzymes
script: The doctor recommends lifestyle adjustments such as diet and exercise. We can schedule a follow-up to reassess.
hard_stop: Keep guidance general unless specific instructions documented.

script: One of your lab values is slightly outside the normal range. The doctor would like to review this with you. It is not urgent, but we recommend scheduling a follow-up visit.
hard_stop: Avoid giving detailed interpretation beyond physician instructions.

script: Your recent lab results have been reviewed and are within normal limits. If you have any questions, we're happy to schedule a follow-up visit.
actions: No automatic visit required unless physician indicated

script: The doctor would like to repeat this lab to confirm the result. I can help schedule your lab appointment.

script: I can schedule your lab appointment now. Do you prefer morning or afternoon?
I'll help you with that. Let me process your request.

script: “To make sure I understand correctly, could you please clarify what you mean?” Offer translated examples if needed.

script: “To ensure accurate communication, we can arrange a professional medical interpreter.” Interpreter Options - phone interpreter service - video interpreter - in-person interpreter

triggers: store English translation in medical record

triggers: patient switches to Spanish or Arabic
script: “I can continue in your preferred language.” Switch response accordingly. Workflow 1️⃣ detect language change 2️⃣ confirm patient preference 3️⃣ update system language context

“Your imaging results have been reviewed. The doctor would like to schedule a visit to discuss the findings.” If patient anxious: “The doctor will explain everything in detail and answer your questions.” Action - Offer appointment - Log patient notified
“To help the doctor prepare, what is the main reason for today’s visit?” “How long has this been going on?”
hard_stop: If red flags appear → alert physician. Action Generate “Doctor Brief” summary.

triggers: Can you explain what you meant
script: Thank you for reaching out. Let me clarify. I will restate the documented plan only. If unclear, I'll confirm with the doctor and update you.

triggers: I have a question about my blood pressure
script: Thank you for your message. I understand your concern. If the answer exists in your documented visit plan, I can provide that. If uncertain, I'll review this with the doctor and follow up shortly.
actions: Route to physician if medical interpretation required

triggers: Can you send me to a cardiologist
script: Thank you. May I ask the reason for the referral? If PPO/Medicare: I'll generate your referral and send it securely. If HMO: This requires insurance authorization. We'll notify you once processed.
I'm glad we were able to address your concern. Please let us know if you need anything further.
I'm reviewing your medication request. If refill status known: Your refill was sent on [date] to [pharmacy]. If pharmacy change: Please confirm the new pharmacy details.

If emergency (chest pain, shortness of breath, stroke, severe bleeding, suicidal thoughts): This may be urgent. Please call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room immediately. If non-emergent: I'm sorry you're experiencing that. When did this start? Are you having fever, severe pain, or worsening symptoms?
actions: If moderate → route to clinical queue
hard_stop: Emergency keywords → immediate 911/ER script; no delay.
